goal: Plan moving day and create a timed plan and checklist for Saturday July 10
Document current rental condition to prevent repaint charges
Coordinate key handoff and pre-move inspection with building manager before moving out
constraints: three flights of stairs; no elevator; narrow stairs
permit may be required for curbside loading
decisions: Not hiring full-service movers; DIY with friends/family/rental truck
Rent 16-foot van; crew of 3 (me + two friends)
Assign roles: Driver/van manager; Lead carrier; Spotter/box manager
Check curb permit requirements for curbside loading
Transport fragile glassware in personal vehicle if possible; otherwise load last and secure on top of load
entities: cat care steps for moving day
special packing instructions for vintage glassware
open: Check forecast night-before and morning-of; build a 1–2 hour buffer into your schedule
Reserve/borrow quick-shelter gear: pop-up canopy (or two), large tarps, and extra moving blankets
Create dry staging area inside the new place near the door
Wrap mattresses and upholstered couch in plastic mattress bags, then blanket-wrap over that to avoid condensation damage
Cover furniture with moving blankets, then tarps or stretch wrap on top for rainproofing; secure with straps or bungee cords
Load items into the van as quickly as possible and keep the rear doors closed except when actively loading/unloading
Stage a covered loading zone at the curb so you can move pieces between van and door without getting everything wet
Lay down cardboard/floor runners and painter’s tape at the top and bottom of stairs to reduce tracking and slipping
Use non-slip gloves and take slower, controlled carries on wet stairs; plan two-person carries for all heavy items
Keep towels and absorbent mats handy to wipe feet, stair treads, and furniture spots quickly
Double-bag important or paper items and place them in sealed plastic bins
Wrap fragile boxes in plastic (contractor bags over the box) and label OPEN FIRST
Secure the load in the van to prevent shifting and keep a dry buffer zone for essentials/fragile boxes

Supplies to grab last-minute: tarps (2–3), pop-up canopy (optional), contractor trash bags (several), moving blankets (6–10), stretch wrap, ratchet/bungee straps, towels, floor runners, painter’s tape, waterproof gloves, zip-top bags
After moving: unwrap and dry items immediately if damp; open boxes in a dry area and air out mattresses/fabrics
Take photos of any damp or damaged items for records
Document damp status and photo records
Pack grandma's vintage glasses using dish-pack or dividers; wrap each glass in acid-free tissue; fill voids; seal and label FRAGILE
If possible, transport the vintage glassware in the car; otherwise keep the dedicated box on top of the load and secure
Photograph packing state and create provenance records for vintage glassware
Label the vintage glassware box clearly and keep it separate from heavy items
Schedule timed key handoff/inspection with building manager before moving day; slot into July 10 plan
